headlines.title: Erdogan Wants Turkey to Have More Babies. Few Parents Are Listening.
headlines.summary: Using cash grants and subsidized loans, President Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s government is trying to increase the birthrate. It’s not working.

headlines.title: Runoff in Peru Offers Two Starkly Differing Visions for the Nation
headlines.summary: The presidential election pits Keiko Fujimori, a conservative, against Roberto Sánchez, a leftist, after a crisis marked by delays and protests.

headlines.title: How Birding Apps Are Boosting Tourism in the World’s Top Bird Destination
headlines.summary: Colombia is home to the most species of birds known to ornithologists. Merlin, an app, is helping to build an “avian tourism” industry there.

headlines.title: In Iraqi Desert, Two Israeli Outposts Were Kept Secret for Months
headlines.summary: Israel spent over a year preparing a covert site in Iraq for its operations against Iran, regional officials say. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base.

headlines.title: W.H.O. Declares Ebola Outbreak a Global Health Emergency
headlines.summary: The agency made the declaration just a day after Africa’s top public health agency first announced the outbreak. There is no approved vaccine for this species of Ebola.

headlines.title: How ISWAP and Boko Haram are reshaping the Lake Chad Basin
Trump warns Iran ‘clock ticking’ on deal; Saudi, UAE report drone attacks
‘Won’t be anything left’: Trump issues threat to Iran amid stalled talks
Israel kills at least five in Lebanon after ‘ceasefire’ extended
Mexican officials announce 10 dead in early-morning shooting in Tehuitzingo
Executions nearly doubled in the U.S. last year, and soared abroad
headlines.summary: The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.

headlines.title: Military aircrew in 'stable condition' following midair collision at Idaho air show
headlines.summary: Two jets appeared to collide and their crews ejected during a performance at an air show at a military base in Idaho. "The aircrew involved in the incident are in stable condition," the base reported.

headlines.title: Trump and administration officials address Christian gathering on National Mall
headlines.summary: The conservative prayer event was organized by Freedom 250, a public-private group working with the White House to organize the celebration of America's 250 birthday this summer.

headlines.title: Bus riders to Montgomery retrace old steps while fighting a new fight
headlines.summary: Thousands of people rallied Saturday in Montgomery, Alabama, to push back against conservative states' efforts to dismantle congressional districts that helped secure Black political representation.

headlines.title: Ukraine conducts large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding 12 others
headlines.summary: One of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russia killed at least four people, including three near Moscow, and wounded a dozen others, local authorities said Sunday.

headlines.title: International experts hail new focus on constructive stability
headlines.summary: The meeting between the leaders of China and the United States in Beijing last week marked another important round of high-level strategic communication between the two countries at a pivotal moment in history.

headlines.title: China pledges active role as WHA opens in Geneva
headlines.summary: The 79th World Health Assembly, or WHA, is set to open on Monday in Geneva, Switzerland, bringing together health officials and representatives from around the world to discuss key global health challenges.

headlines.title: Iran urges intl response to US demands
headlines.summary: Tehran has urged the international community to confront Washington's demands, while the United States warned Iran anew that it will have a "very bad time" if a peace deal is not reached soon.

headlines.title: Old wounds reopened
headlines.summary: Mailen Valle lost her father and two sisters during a hantavirus outbreak more than seven years ago in Epuyen, a village in Argentina's Patagonia region.

headlines.title: Beijing's zero-tariff policy in Africa hailed
headlines.summary: Experts have described China's expanded zero-tariff arrangement for African exports as more than a trade measure, saying the initiative signals a broader shift toward long-term South-South cooperation aimed at strengthening Africa's industrial capacity, regional integration and its role in global value chains.

headlines.title: Why isn't PM sacking Education Minister Pradhan despite repeated paper leaks, asks Rahul
headlines.summary: Rahul Gandhi questions PM Modi's silence on repeated NEET paper leaks and demands the dismissal of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

headlines.title: Throwing non-veg food waste in Ganga could hurt religious sentiments: Allahabad HC on iftar party row
headlines.summary: Allahabad HC rules throwing non-veg waste in Ganga may offend Hindu sentiments, granting bail to accused from iftar party.

headlines.title: Greater Nicobar project a recipe for ecological disaster: Jairam Ramesh
headlines.summary: Jairam Ramesh criticises the Greater Nicobar project as ecologically harmful, urging alternatives to strengthen national defense sustainably.

headlines.title: PM Modi tours iconic Dutch dam; eyes cooperation in water management
headlines.summary: PM Modi visits the Afsluitdijk Dam, seeking enhanced Indo-Dutch cooperation in water management and climate-resilient infrastructure.

headlines.title: Congress leader slams Andhra Pradesh CM’s population growth incentive
headlines.summary: Congress leader Husain Dalwai criticizes Andhra CM's cash incentives for population growth, advocating for better representation instead.
